packageRules.description: Default lane for non-major updates. Split per app below, because one repo-wide batch pooled five unrelated breakages (a genai field, a new stylelint rule, an oxfmt reformat, two typecheck breaks) into a single 2,300-line PR that couldn't be reviewed or bisected. Per-app groups stay small enough to fix in one sitting.
Desktop app deps, Rust and JS together (a specta bump regenerates bindings.ts, so they have to land as one).
Worker + dashboard deps: both are Cloudflare-targeted and share workers-types.
Linters and formatters land on their own: a version bump here rewrites or re-flags tracked files across every app, which is noise to review mixed in with real dependency changes.
Group all major updates into one monthly PR
schedule: before 6am on the first day of the month

packageRules.description: Security patches get immediate PRs, auto-merge
First-party crates (David's own). Stricter pre-release gate than the 3-day window protects against; bypass so cmdr pulls fixes immediately.

The specta trio stays on rc.24 / 0.0.11. rc.25 types every plain `f32`/`f64` as `number | null`. On return values that's defensible (serde_json renders NaN/Infinity as null), but it does the same to PARAMETERS, where it's wrong: `viewer_get_lines(target_value: f64)` and the four `media_index_*_threshold` commands take non-Option floats and serde rejects a JSON null for them. Adopting it trades a latent, never-observed hazard for a live one, plus ~25 sites of null-handling. Rationale: `apps/desktop/src-tauri/DETAILS.md` § Number types over IPC. Re-evaluate on the next rc; bump all three together or not at all.
rand_core stays 0.6.x: the test-only keygen must match ed25519-dalek 2.x's rand_core API, and the `getrandom` feature is gone in later versions, so a bump can't resolve.
The SMB benchmark harness pins sspi and smb-rpc in lockstep with smb 0.11's internal pins; independent bumps can never resolve, so leave the whole harness alone.

The CLIP → Core ML conversion pins record which versions built the SHA-256-pinned model artifacts on Hugging Face, so they're provenance, not a dependency set. coremltools declares no torch/transformers bound, meaning the resolver validates nothing; the only real check is re-running convert.py and comparing fidelity cosines. A bot bump would be drift at best and a silently broken script at worst. See the file's header.
